name: Yala National Park
description: Explore the lush jungles and teeming wildlife of Yala National Park, a sanctuary where elephants, leopards, and other primates roam freely amidst verdant landscapes. This protected area offers a unique glimpse into Sri Lanka’s natural heritage, making it a must-visit destination for nature lovers and wildlife enthusiasts.
mobile_signal: Good near main entrance; weak inside park
activities: Jeep safaris, Elephant watching, Bird watching
ticket_price: USD 25-45 (foreign adult) + service charge + 18% VAT; LKR ~60 (local adult)
wheelchair_access: limited - mostly jeep safari based; visitor center is accessible
monsoon_note: Block 1 sometimes closes briefly during heavy NE monsoon (Oct-Jan) flooding; park stays open most of year

name: Wilpattu National Park
description: Wilpattu National Park, a vast wilderness sanctuary in Puttalam, North Western Province, offers a unique blend of untouched nature, ancient ruins, and diverse wildlife. This expansive park is home to over 100 species of birds, including the elusive leopard, and its unique soda lakes, known as villus, provide a fascinating glimpse into Sri Lanka’s natural heritage.
mobile_signal: Weak to moderate inside park; better near entrance

name: Horton Plains National Park
description: Discover the Horton Plains National Park, a pristine highland sanctuary in Central Province, Sri Lanka, known for its diverse wildlife, ancient tea plantations, and breathtaking panoramic views. This UNESCO Global Geopark offers a unique blend of nature and culture, perfect for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike.
activities: Bird Watching, Tea Plantation Tours, Nature Photography

name: Udawalawe National Park
description: Udawalawe National Park is a sanctuary for Sri Lanka’s elephants, offering breathtaking landscapes and a chance to witness these magnificent creatures in their natural habitat. Located in Ratnapura, Sabaragamuwa province, it’s a haven for nature lovers and wildlife enthusiasts.
mobile_signal: Good near entrance and Elephant Transit Home
activities: Elephant watching, bird watching, nature walks
ticket_price: USD 42.5 (foreign adult) including taxes; LKR ~60 (local adult)
wheelchair_access: elephant transit home viewing deck is wheelchair accessible
monsoon_note: Some interior tracks flood during NE monsoon (Oct-Jan); reservoir levels rise, improving elephant sightings near shoreline

name: Wasgamuwa National Park
description: Discover Wasgamuwa National Park, a verdant sanctuary teeming with diverse wildlife, including the elusive Sri Lankan leopard. Spanning over 10,000 hectares, this protected area offers a glimpse into Sri Lanka’s ancient forest ecosystems, perfect for nature enthusiasts seeking a tranquil escape.
mobile_signal: Weak inside park; moderate near entrance
activities: Bird watching, jungle trekking, wildlife spotting

name: Gal Oya National Park
description: Gal Oya National Park, nestled in the Eastern Province, offers a serene retreat where nature lovers can explore lush forests, crystal-clear streams, and the majestic Senanayake Samudra Reservoir. This protected area provides a perfect escape from the hustle of daily life.
mobile_signal: Weak inside park; moderate near Inginiyagala
activities: Boat safaris, jungle treks, bird watching
wheelchair_access: limited - boat safari and jeep-based tours
monsoon_note: Senanayake Samudra reservoir levels rise during NE monsoon (Oct-Jan), improving boat safari views; some tracks muddy

name: Bundala National Park
description: Bundala National Park, a sanctuary for over 200,000 migratory birds, offers breathtaking views of flooded lagoons and lush wetlands. This protected area is a haven for birdwatchers and nature enthusiasts, with its diverse ecosystem attracting visitors from around the world.

name: Kumana National Park
description: Kumana National Park, a sanctuary of nature and wildlife, offers breathtaking landscapes and a rich ecosystem teeming with birds and aquatic life. Located in Ampara district, it's a haven for birdwatchers and nature enthusiasts, perfect for those seeking a tranquil escape from the hustle of everyday life.
food_nearby: very limited - nearest options in arugam bay/pottuvil

name: Kaudulla National Park
description: Kaudulla National Park is a stunning wildlife sanctuary in Polonnaruwa, North Central Province, known for its annual elephant gatherings. Home to over 10,000 elephants, it offers a unique glimpse into Sri Lanka’s natural heritage, making it a must-visit for nature enthusiasts.
activities: Elephant watching, bird watching, cultural tours
ticket_price: USD 40 (foreign adult) including taxes; LKR ~60 (local adult)
monsoon_note: Best Aug-Dec during elephant gathering; tank levels and access vary seasonally

name: Minneriya National Park
description: Discover the mesmerizing Minneriya National Park, where up to 5,000 elephants gather in the dry season, creating a spectacle of nature. This sanctuary offers a glimpse into the ancient life cycle of these majestic creatures.
activities: Elephant watching, bird watching, cultural tours
ticket_price: USD 40 (foreign adult) including taxes; LKR ~60 (local adult)
monsoon_note: Famous 'Gathering' of elephants Jul-Sep dry season; less crowded but wetter Oct-Jan

name: Maduru Oya National Park
description: Maduru Oya National Park is a serene sanctuary nestled in the Eastern Province, known for its lush landscapes, diverse wildlife, and ancient ruins. This protected area offers visitors a chance to explore nature while learning about the region's rich cultural heritage.
activities: Bird watching, jungle trekking, cultural tours

name: Lunugamvehera National Park
description: Lunugamvehera National Park, nestled in the heart of Southern Sri Lanka, offers a sanctuary for nature enthusiasts and wildlife lovers. This lush park is a vital elephant corridor, connecting Yala National Park and serving as a crucial habitat for over 100 species of birds and mammals.
activities: Bird Watching, Elephant Watching, Nature Walks

name: Angammedilla National Park
description: Angammedilla National Park, nestled in the lush Polonnaruwa district, offers a serene retreat where nature lovers can explore its diverse flora and fauna while avoiding the flooding risks of the Parakrama Samudra catchment area.
activities: Bird watching, Nature walks, Cultural tours

name: Flood Plains National Park
description: Explore the Flood Plains National Park, a verdant sanctuary where the Mahaweli River’s seasonal floods create a dynamic ecosystem. Home to diverse wildlife and lush landscapes, it offers a unique glimpse into Sri Lanka's natural heritage.
activities: Bird watching, nature photography, elephant viewing

name: Somawathiya National Park
description: Discover the ancient ruins and lush landscapes of Somawathiya National Park, a hidden gem in Polonnaruwa. This serene park offers a glimpse into Sri Lanka’s rich cultural heritage and natural beauty, perfect for nature lovers and history buffs alike.

name: Horowpathana National Park
description: Horowpathana National Park, nestled in the lush Anuradhapura district, offers visitors a sanctuary for over 100 species of birds and a chance to witness the majestic elephants roaming freely under the watchful eye of conservationists.
activities: Bird watching, Elephant safaris, Nature photography
monsoon_note: One of the newer parks for elephant conservation; access roads can be muddy Oct-Dec

name: Adam's Bridge National Park
description: Explore Adam's Bridge National Park, a unique marine sanctuary where ancient sand dunes meet the Indian Ocean. This protected area offers a glimpse into nature’s intricate balance, perfect for birdwatchers and nature enthusiasts.

name: Chundikkulam National Park
description: Chundikkulam National Park, nestled along the coast, offers a unique blend of coastal wetlands and scenic views. This protected area is a haven for bird watchers, with its prime location attracting migratory species during the NE monsoon season.

name: Delft National Park
description: Delft National Park, located on the Jaffna Peninsula, offers a serene retreat with its lush greenery, ancient ruins, and tranquil waterfalls. This protected area is a haven for nature lovers and historians, featuring well-preserved temples and archaeological sites alongside breathtaking natural landscapes.

name: Galway's Land National Park
description: Galway's Land National Park is a stunning highland retreat in Nuwara Eliya, offering breathtaking views, lush forests, and serene hiking trails. This picturesque park is perfect for nature lovers seeking peace and tranquility amidst the cool, misty landscapes.

name: Hikkaduwa National Park
description: Discover the vibrant marine life at Hikkaduwa National Park, a hidden gem where colorful coral reefs and diverse marine species await. Nestled along the Galle coast, this protected area offers a perfect blend of natural beauty and recreational activities.
activities: Snorkeling, Glass-bottom Boat Tours, Beach Relaxation
wheelchair_access: limited beach access; some viewing points accessible

name: Horagolla National Park
description: Horagolla National Park, nestled in the Western Province, offers lush landscapes, diverse wildlife, and serene hiking trails. This protected area is perfect for nature enthusiasts seeking a peaceful escape from the hustle of daily life.
ticket_price: LKR 500-1000 approx small park entry; confirm at entrance

name: Lahugala Kitulana National Park
description: Lahugala Kitulana National Park, a lush sanctuary nestled in the Eastern Province, offers visitors a chance to explore its diverse wildlife and natural beauty. Home to elephants, leopards, and various bird species, it’s a haven for nature lovers and photographers alike.
ticket_price: LKR 40 + LKR 300 group fee (local); USD 10 + USD 8 group fee (foreign) + 15% VAT
food_nearby: limited - shops in nearby pottuvil/lahugala town

name: Madhu Road National Park
description: Madhu Road National Park, nestled along the Mannar coast, offers a unique blend of untouched nature, historical significance, and ecological diversity. This park is a sanctuary for various wildlife and a haven for nature enthusiasts seeking tranquility and adventure.
activities: Bird watching, Nature photography, Jungle safaris

name: Ussangoda National Park
description: Ussangoda National Park stands as a unique coastal sanctuary, offering pristine beaches, ancient ruins, and breathtaking views. Its sandy shores and lush greenery provide a serene retreat from the hustle of daily life.
activities: Beach Relaxation, Coastal Scenic Walk, Archaeological Exploration

name: Pigeon Island National Park
description: Pigeon Island National Park, a hidden gem off Trincomalee’s eastern coast, offers pristine beaches, crystal-clear waters, and a rich marine ecosystem. This UNESCO site is perfect for snorkeling, diving, and exploring its coral reefs, while the island’s lush vegetation and historical significance make it a must-visit for nature lovers and history buffs alike.

name: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
description: Explore Sinharaja Forest Reserve, a UNESCO World Heritage site and Sri Lanka's wettest forest, where ancient trees and diverse wildlife thrive amidst lush greenery and cool mist.
activities: Bird watching, nature photography, jungle trekking
ticket_price: LKR 1000 (local adult) approx; USD 20-25 (foreign adult) approx including guide fee
food_nearby: limited small shops/guesthouses near kudawa entrance

name: Ritigala Strict Nature Reserve
description: Ritigala Strict Nature Reserve is a sacred Buddhist site and ancient ruins nestled amidst lush forests, offering visitors a glimpse into Sri Lanka's rich cultural and natural heritage. This UNESCO World Heritage Site features impressive ruins, monasteries, and ancient temples dating back centuries, surrounded by verdant hills and serene streams.
ticket_price: Entry restricted - special permit required from DWC
monsoon_note: Access already restricted; monsoon rains (Oct-Jan) further limit trail conditions

name: Hakgala Strict Nature Reserve
description: Discover Hakgala Strict Nature Reserve, a lush botanical gem nestled in the Central Province’s highlands. Spanning over 100 acres, this sanctuary showcases a diverse array of flora and fauna, including rare orchids and exotic birds. Its serene beauty and tranquil atmosphere make it a perfect retreat for nature lovers and hikers alike.
monsoon_note: Highland climate, misty and cool year-round; heaviest rain Apr-Jun and Oct-Nov

name: Kalametiya Sanctuary
description: Kalametiya Sanctuary, a lush wetland oasis in Hambantota, offers visitors a chance to observe over 200 species of birds, including the elusive Malayan Pied Hornbill. This important bird sanctuary is a haven for nature lovers and birdwatchers, with its lagoon levels peaking in the dry season, making it a must-visit spot.
wheelchair_access: limited - accessible only at the lagoon viewing area
monsoon_note: Important bird sanctuary; lagoon levels and bird activity peak in dry season Mar-Sep

name: Bellanwila-Attidiya Sanctuary
description: Bellanwila-Attidiya Sanctuary, a lush urban wetland nestled in Colombo’s heart, offers visitors a tranquil escape with its diverse flora and fauna, perfect for nature lovers and families seeking a peaceful retreat amidst the bustling city.
ticket_price: LKR 500 approx (combined visitor fee); confirm at entrance
monsoon_note: Urban wetland; flooding risk during heavy SW monsoon rains (May-Sep) is a known local issue

name: Anawilundawa Sanctuary
description: Anawilundawa Sanctuary, a Ramsar wetland and important migratory bird habitat, offers visitors a chance to witness nature at its finest. Located in Puttalam, this sanctuary is a haven for bird watchers and nature enthusiasts, with its diverse ecosystem and tranquil environment.
ticket_price: No formal ticket - open access Ramsar wetland; donations welcome

name: Vankalai Sanctuary
description: Vankalai Sanctuary, nestled amidst the lush jungles of Mannar, is a haven for nature enthusiasts and bird watchers. This protected area is a key wintering site for migratory waterbirds, offering visitors a chance to witness the spectacle of thousands of birds gathering in the sanctuary during the monsoon season.
activities: Bird watching, photography, exploring the sanctuary
ticket_price: No formal ticket - open access sanctuary
food_nearby: very limited - shops in mannar town
monsoon_note: Key wintering site for migratory waterbirds; NE monsoon (Oct-Jan) coincides with peak bird season

name: Giant's Tank Sanctuary
description: Discover Giant's Tank Sanctuary, a serene wetland oasis in Mannar, Northern Province. This protected area teems with diverse bird species, including the iconic Painted Stork, and offers visitors a chance to witness nature at its finest without the need for formal entry fees.
food_nearby: very limited - shops in mannar town
monsoon_note: Tank levels rise with NE monsoon rains (Oct-Jan), attracting waterbirds; access roads can be muddy

name: Kataragama Sanctuary
description: Kataragama Sanctuary, a sacred site for devotees of the Hindu deity Kataragama, offers a blend of ancient rituals and modern offerings. This unique sanctuary is surrounded by the bustling town of Kataragama, where visitors can immerse themselves in its rich cultural heritage and vibrant atmosphere.
wheelchair_access: most areas accessible, but the inner sanctum may require assistance
monsoon_note: Heavy rainfall during May to September

name: Wirawila-Tissa Sanctuary
description: Wirawila-Tissa Sanctuary is a serene sanctuary nestled in the Southern Province, known for its diverse wildlife and lush greenery. This protected area offers visitors a chance to observe birds and other native species in their natural habitat, making it a must-visit spot for nature enthusiasts.
wheelchair_access: limited - accessible via tank bund path

name: Peak Wilderness Sanctuary
description: Peak Wilderness Sanctuary, also known as the Adam's Peak Sanctuary, is a sacred site nestled among lush forests and dramatic cliffs. This UNESCO World Heritage site is renowned for its spiritual significance and breathtaking views, attracting both pilgrims and nature enthusiasts seeking a unique hiking experience.
mobile_signal: Weak on the climb; moderate at base
ticket_price: Sri Pada (Adam's Peak) pilgrimage path: no formal ticket; donations at shrine

name: Udawatta Kele Sanctuary
description: Udawatta Kele Sanctuary, a lush oasis in the Central Province, offers visitors a glimpse into Sri Lanka’s rich biodiversity. This sanctuary, known for its diverse flora and fauna, is a haven for nature lovers and wildlife enthusiasts. Nestled amidst the verdant hills, it provides a tranquil retreat from the hustle of city life.

name: Knuckles Conservation Forest
description: Explore the Knuckles Conservation Forest, a lush, misty haven where ancient trees whisper secrets of the past. Home to diverse wildlife and endemic species, this forest offers a unique glimpse into Sri Lanka’s natural heritage.

name: Kanneliya-Dediyagala-Nakiyadeniya (KDN) Forest Reserve
description: Discover the lush wet zone rainforest of Kanneliya-Dediyagala-Nakiyadeniya (KDN) Forest Reserve, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve where ancient trees and diverse wildlife thrive amidst the verdant hills and streams.
mobile_signal: Weak inside forest; moderate near entrance
